text:
SAINT QUENTIN-EN-YVELINES, France 芒聙聯 The French and English might not always be on the best of terms, famously known for not getting along, especially when it comes to sports.

But Tommy Fleetwood said Hideki Matsuyama jokingly wondered about his nationality given all the support the Englishman was getting on Friday from a predominantly French audience during the second round of the Olympic Golf Tournament.

芒聙聹It's been great,芒聙聶芒聙聶 said Fleetwood, who despite a bogey on the final hole holds a share of the lead with Matsuyama and Xander Schauffele. 芒聙聹The crowd has been absolutely amazing. It's a different set of supporters than what we get week-in and week-out.

芒聙聹It's a very Olympic crowd and atmosphere and it's been great. I always consider myself very lucky with the support I get but they have been amazing out there. Wyndham (Clark) and Hideki, both芒聙聰so that was sweet. They have been great with me.芒聙聶芒聙聶

Shop: Get the best prices on golf rangefinders through SI Golf's retail partners

Fleetwood芒聙聶s 7-under-par 64 put him in position to earn a medal this weekend at Le Golf National, where the PGA Tour and DP World Tour won player the French Open in 2017 and starred in the Ryder Cup in 2018.

His only blemish was that bogey on 18, a hole that promises to be interesting over the weekend. The hole has caused it芒聙聶s share of fits. Matsuyama made a double-bogey there to fall into a tie with Schauffele, who added a 66 tohis opening 65.

芒聙聹It was a sad ending, but I'm glad that today was only the second day,芒聙聶芒聙聶 said Matsuyama, the 2021 Masters champion who missed out on a bronze medal in Japan three years ago when he lost in a playoff. 芒聙聹I think it means I need to change my mindset for the third day. I was glad I was able to get birdies.芒聙聶芒聙聶

That trio has played 36 holes in 131, 11 under par on a relatively benign course that is playing soft. They will be in the same group that tees off at 12:39 p.m. local time (6:39 a.m. ET) on Saturday.

Jon Rahm, with a 66, pulled within two shots of the leaders. Belgium芒聙聶s Thomas Detry, Chinese Taipei芒聙聶s C.T. Pan (who won bronze in 2021) and South Korea芒聙聶s Tom Kim are another shot back.

Scottie Scheffler is on the fringe of contention in a tie for 10th脗聽and five strokes behind the leaders while Rory McIlroy will have to come from six behind.

芒聙聹It's a great atmosphere to play in,芒聙聶芒聙聶 McIlroy said. 芒聙聹It's awesome, just the enthusiasm of the crowd is great.芒聙聶芒聙聶

There are 18 players within six shots of the lead.

Not much has gotten in Schauffele芒聙聶s way of late, so perhaps he was due for some interference from nature.

A pile of ants, of all things, sent Schauffele on his way to a rare bogey. And not even that managed to knock him out of a share of the lead.

Schauffele, the reigning gold medal winner fresh off a win at the British Open, encountered his issue at the par-4 13th脗聽hole where he hit his ball in the rough and noticed something behind it.

芒聙聹I was kind of putting my club down and sort of that fluffy sort of芒聙聰fluffy grass almost, and I'm taking practice swings and I'm trying to get to my ball and I can't even . . . what's going on, something is behind my ball.

芒聙聹I went down and saw a pile, so I called an official. It was just ants. It was a pile of ants, an ant pile, or whatever you want to call it, and their home; so I didn't want to mess with it. Couldn't take relief. It's a loose impediment.芒聙聶芒聙聶

Schauffele sought the advise of two rules officials who said he could not be given a drop in that situation but could remove the ants from behind the ball.

芒聙聹I didn't do anything to be honest,芒聙聶芒聙聶 Schauffele said. 芒聙聹I ended up hacking out 50 yards on the fairway.芒聙聶芒聙聶

That led to his second bogey of the day but he rebounded with a birdie at the next hole and parred his way in.

芒聙聹When you're in good form, all of us know that it's all about just making the correct decision and executing the shot and rinse and repeating that process,芒聙聶芒聙聶 Schauffele said. 芒聙聹So don't let too much try and bother you, and if you do hit a bad shots, it's easier to accept it because you know you're playing really good golf and you can make it up somewhere else. In that sort of zone, I guess.芒聙聶芒聙聶

For Fleetwood, an Olympic victory could be considered the best of his career. He has yet to win on the PGA Tour and has come close in a few major championships. He's currently ranked 14th脗聽in the world and won in January at the Dubai Invitational. But he has just two top-10s since, one of them a tie for third at the Masters.

芒聙聹I actually haven't been in contention for a while,芒聙聶芒聙聶 Fleetwood said. 芒聙聹I kind of enjoy that on its own, as well as it being the Olympics.

芒聙聹Like I say, it brings with it its own mental challenges. You're playing for a medal. You look at the atmosphere this week, we constantly get asked the question, where does the Olympics sit in golf. When you look at how it's going this week, it continues and continues to grow, and what people feel about it. It brings its own nerves and excitement and it's very, very special. So it's its own unique feeling and it's amazing.芒聙聶芒聙聶

Bob Harig is a senior writer covering golf for Sports Illustrated. He has more than 25 years experience on the beat, including 15 at ESPN. Harig is a regular guest on Sirius XM PGA Tour Radio and has written two books, 芒聙聹DRIVE: The Lasting Legacy of Tiger Woods芒聙聺 and 芒聙聹Tiger and Phil: Golf芒聙聶s Most Fascinating Rivalry.芒聙聺 He graduated from Indiana University where he earned an Evans Scholarship, named in honor of the great amateur golfer Charles (Chick) Evans Jr. Harig, a former president of the Golf Writers Association of America, lives in Clearwater, Fla.

title: Schauffele, Fleetwood and Matsuyama Share Lead As Impassioned Fans Add Energy to Olympic Golf
description: Schauffele is the defending gold medalist, but the event is wide open entering the weekend, as there are 18 players within six shots of the lead, including Rory McIlroy and Scottie Scheffler.
